post_data.content:
At Yogabar, yoga isn't just at the heart of our name; it's at the heart of everything we do. It drives the food we create, the community we celebrate, and the purpose we show up with every day.

This International Day of Yoga, we asked people across the country what yoga means to them, and every single answer reminded us just how boundless this practice truly is. And when we tried to capture it, we realized it's impossible to fit something as infinite as yoga under a minute. 

Yoga is Mind. Body. Soul. Yoga is infinite.✨

It cannot be contained. It stretches as far as the people who practice it, and there are no two journeys that look the same. Here's to yoga in all its forms. Happy International Day of Yoga🧘🏻‍♀️

What does yoga mean to you? Tell us in the comments.

#yogabar #yogaday #internationaldayofyoga

author.name: Hemant Malik
author.info: Whole time Director ITC Limited, Divisional Chief Executive- Foods,

postedAt.postedAgoText: 2 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

repost.content:
| On Sunbean Coffee Conversations |

The passage of my work day is now almost ritualistic
The dexterity with which my fingers move across the laptop keyboard
The steps I take to and fro the pantry
The cabin doors I knock on
The rhythm of conversations that I have
I think I almost know what will make me sigh, what will lead to a crease on my temples and what will make me smile

But what I did not know was how to react to the mail titled "Sunbean Coffee Conversations"
I skimmed through it, like one does, assuming to telepathically arrive at the answer, but I still did not get it.
What could churn up from a conversation between the 'Whole Time Director ITC Limited, Divisional Chief Executive- Foods' and someone who is just a couple of years in the organisation?

I marked the message unread
And after a bout of what they call BAU, I went back to the mail and did what one does, sent it to my manager asking what it is. And as a manager does he told me, it'll be good for you, you'll figure it out.

And so I did, I did my share of Linkedin stalking, read up what I could and when the HR told me it's just an informal conversation - I put my mind to rest, a yapper's got to yap, and that she will :)

And when the moment came, I and a few of my peers were greeted with Ruchika Asthana's and Hemant Malik Sir's warm smile (I really want to say beaming/beaning smile but I shall save the pun for later) and the whiff of coffee in the air, beckoning us to be ourselves. 

The thing about being in a room of peers along with someone far more experienced on the table - is the urge to want to make the most sense, say something that sticks, shine even. What a cynical urge, Narcissus would be elated. But I'm glad that, I and my peers who were a part of the conversation were miles away from that cynical puddle as we spoke about our tales, got to listen to so many of them, looked at each other with sometimes scrunched eyebrows and sometimes a shy smile. 

Like puzzle pieces we put together how we see things change, what could become of us and this organisation, we had ideas, some contrary opinions all simmering together for us to be left with sufficiently scrumptious food for thought (see what I did there?)

When I went back to my desk, I was ever so delightfully able to re look at my aforementioned ritual of being at work. I was no longer just praying the rosary but looking carefully at every bead and appreciating it - my conversations with peers, the back and forth with my team and even the mails that I was typing away. They were a lot more purposeful or let me say it was as if a ray of sunshine had braided itself into everything and boy, does that make working more fun. I guess that is the effect a good conversation can have on you, or more precisely that is the effect of a "Sunbean Coffee Conversation"


Fin.

For me personally, the launch of Bingo! was a master class in brand building. Something I still go back to as I now sit on the other side of the table, as brand lead at Dinshaw's Dairy Foods Pvt Ltd. Decades have passed, we now work the algorithm, use AI, inhabit meme verses, juice influencers etc. but some rules of the game, do not change. No guts, no glory. 

And it often takes a village…Hemant Malik Amit Akali Sendil Kumar Rajesh Krishnan 🙌

Dinshaw's Dairy Foods Pvt Ltd 
Zervin Rana

author.name: Malvika Mehra
author.info: Creative Head, Marketing, Dinshaw’s Dairy Foods I Former Chief Creative Officer, The Good Glamm Group I Dentsu I Grey I Ogilvy I Founder, Tomorrow Creative Lab

postedAt.postedAgoText: 2 months ago • Edited • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

repost.content:
Brave ideas are always remembered #GoodAdsMatterRoundtable

At the Good Ads Matter Chief Creative Officers Roundtable, Malvika Mehra, Independent Creative Director & Creative Head of Marketing at Dinshaw's Dairy Foods Pvt Ltd, opened up about the conception of the launch campaign for ITC Bingo project she was involved in, during her time at Ogilvy.

At a time when PepsiCo’s Lays dominated the market, the team had to find a way to break through. They developed a bold, unconventional campaign that didn’t follow the usual playbook. When presented to Piyush Pandey, he wasn’t sure if the films would go through. The sales team at Bingo strongly pushed back as well, creating a high-pressure situation, with just three days left for the campaign to go live.

The brand team decided to take the risk and launch the campaign, which was met with huge success.

Panel Moderator: Mayur Hola

Want to hear more from India’s Top 1% Chief Creative Officers?

Watch the full Rountable here: https://lnkd.in/dGC7SYJp

postedAt.postedAgoText: 2 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

post_data.content:
Hidden Hunger…. We might be consuming sufficient calories, but the lack of Micro Nutrients (Vit A, Iron, Iodine, Folate, Zinc) leave our bodies deprived of its needs. Today is National Anemia Day, large part of us Indians remain deficient in Iron, the deficiency is across income class and age, girls and women are even more vulnerable. 

Iodized Salt has been a very successful solution for ensuring the problem of iodine deficiency is solved. 
Why can’t Salt also deliver Iron ? 
The challenge - Double Fortified Salt is available, however the Ferrous compound oxidises and the salt color becomes yellow, Consumer feels suspicious about the quality of salt and hence the adoption of Double Fortified Salt is very low.

ITC’s solution - Aashirvaad Iron Shakti Iodized Salt. Our scientists at LSTC have successfully encapsulated the ferrous fumarate. These are now visible as small blue specks of iron boosters in the salt. Since the consumer can see these specks, they are more convinced about the efficacy. Aashirvaad Iron Shakti provides 25% of the average daily iron requirement while ensuring no change in the taste of food. (Assuming the 5g per day consumption of Salt as per WHO) 

This empowers families to boost their iron intake daily, with minimal habit shift. Double-fortified salt is demonstrated in research to improve iron levels, making it a powerful tool to meet daily iron needs.

At ITC we are also actively engaging with stakeholders and experts to advance meaningful dialogue and collective action on iron deficiency. Through our CSR initiatives, Maternal & Child Health & Nutrition and Project Samposhan, we are strengthening community health ecosystem and nutrition awareness on anaemia, iron deficiency, iron-rich foods. Launched in 2021, Project Samposhan has touched the lives of over 1.5 lakh beneficiaries, driving meaningful progress in the fight against anaemia.
ITC Limited Nazeeb Arif Anuj Rustagi Agatha Betsy, PhD Nupur Agarrwal Shantanu Das Dr. Bhavna Sharma

post_data.content:
Who owns the Brand - CEO vs CMO. I was happy to share my views in ET Brand Equity’s 1st ‘Split Screen’ debate. Congrats Sonali Krishna Editor BE for the complete new look and relaunch of our once favorite Brand Equity.
Very good points Kalpana Rangamani I agree on the value of ‘that mad imagination of a CMO’ for being able to bring that distinctiveness to the brand and for the CEO’s to be the counter balance; and if I can add for the ‘CEO to not stifle it and to encourage some risk taking’.
To me ‘Brand is the Soul of the Business’, each person in the organisation, across functions, has a role towards building the brand; but finally Brand is the CEO’s responsibility.

ITC Limited Nazeeb Arif Shuvadip Banerjee Subash Balar Komal Vashist 
 Marketing: Who Truly Owns the Brand? The CEO vs. CMO Debate Unveiled, ETBrandEquity https://lnkd.in/gd-XuPwY

document.title: Who owns the Brand CEO or CMO

post_data.content:
Trust your Gut Instinct…It might be wiser than you thought !! How do you build your Gut….
Science now recognises the role your Gut has on how you feel, how you look, think and act each day. Our changing lifestyle demands higher concern for our Gut.
On this World Digestive Day, let’s take a pause and reflect on the health of our Gut.

Aashirvaad Happy Tummy platform shows that 80% of Indians are Not Aware of their daily fibre requirements and 70% are not consuming the required amounts ! This is a large gap.
As I look through our portfolio of products from ITC Foods, supporting gut health, I see many strong offers, in formats that are easy to consume and for every day use across staples, snacks and milk.
Providing Whole grain and fibre nutrition through Aashirvaad Atta with Multigrains, Right Shift Oats++, snacks like Sunfeast Farmlite biscuits, Yoga Bar with unique patented combinations of pre & probiotics with enzymes & herbal blends for gut friendly protein intakes.
Aashirvaad Svasti Easy Digest Milk addresses a very real consumer discomfort by offering a lactose-free milk option. 
I can see many more products coming to the market soon.

“Today on This World Digestive Health Day, we reaffirm our commitment of continued offerings of gut-friendly solutions developed by evolved understanding through research and development, collaboration with experts and consumer awareness through scientific publications and community initiatives to build digestive health as a strategic platform.”.

ITC Limited Nazeeb Arif Agatha Betsy, PhD Shantanu Das Krishna Joshi Nupur Agarrwal 
Nandini Basu Aurko Dasgupta Somnath Chatterjee

post_data.content:
This is my favorite award !  ITC awarded the Best Companies For Women (Avtar & Seramount).

I have often been queried about ‘traditional organisations’ approach on DEI - hopefully not any more.

I had posted about our DEI journey a couple of months back and the effort put in by my team has indeed been world class.
All credit to the HR team, to the DEI council and the DEI change maker cohorts.

The happiness and the pride in the picture of the team that collected the award says it all !

Amitav Mukherji P J Anand Ruchir Jhingran Nikhil Moza Ruchika Asthana Priyanka Chwal Nazeeb Arif ITC Limited Avtar - Leaders in Workplace Culture & Inclusion Seramount
